title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: vignette presents comprehensive benchmark results mlr3learners.gpytorch package, comparing GPyTorch Gaussian Process regression standard algorithms.

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: Two regression datasets used evaluation: airquality: 111 observations, predicting Ozone levels boston: 506 observations, predicting median house values

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: 5-fold cross-validation following learners: GPyTorch (CPU) GPyTorch (GPU) K-Nearest Neighbors (auto-tuned k=1-30) CV-Glmnet (penalized linear regression) Featureless baseline

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: airquality dataset: KNN: 388.28 MSE GPyTorch: 398.58 MSE (64% improvement baseline) CV-Glmnet: 575.86 MSE Featureless: 1107.45 MSE boston dataset: GPyTorch: 9.68 MSE (89% improvement baseline) KNN: 18.02 MSE CV-Glmnet: 27.79 MSE Featureless: 84.70 MSE

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: Tested NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU.

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: airquality (111 observations): CPU: 0.19-0.24 seconds GPU: 0.31-0.48 seconds Result: CPU 2x faster (GPU overhead dominates) boston (506 observations): CPU: 0.34-0.37 seconds GPU: 0.28-0.33 seconds Result: GPU 1.3x faster

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: GPU overhead (memory transfer) approximately 0.2 seconds. small datasets, overhead exceeds computational gains. larger datasets (500+ observations), GPU acceleration becomes beneficial.

title: Benchmark Results: GPyTorch Learner
text: GPyTorch demonstrates: Excellent learning capability (64-89% improvement baseline) Competitive performance (rank 1 boston, rank 2 airquality) GPU acceleration beneficial datasets 500+ observations Identical predictions CPU GPU (validates implementation) See main README complete details.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Gaussian Process regression learner mlr3 using GPyTorch GPU acceleration support.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: mlr3 (>= 0.20.0) reticulate R6 paradox

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Python (>= 3.7) PyTorch (CUDA support GPU acceleration) GPyTorch

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
code: conda create -n gpytorch python=3.10 conda activate gpytorch conda install pytorch -c pytorch pip install gpytorch

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
code: conda create -n gpytorch python=3.10 conda activate gpytorch pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118 pip install gpytorch

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
code: library(mlr3) library(mlr3learners.gpytorch) library(reticulate)  use_condaenv("gpytorch")  task <- tsk("mtcars") learner <- lrn("regr.gpytorch", n_iter = 50)  learner$train(task) pred <- learner$predict(task) print(pred)

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
code: learner_gpu <- lrn("regr.gpytorch", n_iter = 50, device = "cuda") learner_cpu <- lrn("regr.gpytorch", n_iter = 50, device = "cpu") learner_auto <- lrn("regr.gpytorch", n_iter = 50, device = "auto")

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Performance evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE) 5-fold cross-validation.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Lower MSE values indicate better performance.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: airquality dataset (111 observations) KNN: 388.28 MSE GPyTorch (CPU/GPU): 398.58 MSE CV-Glmnet: 575.86 MSE Featureless: 1107.45 MSE boston dataset (506 observations) GPyTorch (CPU/GPU): 9.68 MSE KNN: 18.02 MSE CV-Glmnet: 27.79 MSE Featureless: 84.70 MSE

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Question 1: GPyTorch learn non-trivial patterns? airquality: 64.01% improvement featureless baseline boston: 88.57% improvement featureless baseline Yes, GPyTorch successfully learns non-trivial patterns datasets. Question 2: GPyTorch competitive algorithms? Achieves rank 1 boston dataset Achieves rank 2 airquality dataset Outperforms linear models (CV-Glmnet) datasets Highly competitive KNN GPyTorch demonstrates excellent performance, particularly larger boston dataset.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Performance comparison conducted NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: GPU CPU produce identical prediction accuracy (MSE): airquality: 398.58 (GPU) = 398.58 (CPU) boston: 9.68 (GPU) = 9.68 (CPU) confirms correct implementation across hardware backends.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: airquality dataset (111 observations) boston dataset (506 observations)

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Small datasets (fewer 200 observations): CPU faster due GPU memory transfer overhead. time required transfer data CPU GPU memory exceeds computational speedup. Medium datasets (500-1000 observations): GPU begins show advantages computational workload increases relative transfer overhead. Large datasets (1000+ observations): GPU provides significant speedup (expected 2-10x) computational benefits outweigh transfer costs. Recommendation: Use CPU small datasets prototyping. Use GPU production workloads large datasets training many models.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: airquality dataset (111 observations), CPU approximately 2x faster GPU due memory transfer overhead CPU GPU (approximately 0.2 seconds) exceeding computational gain. boston dataset (506 observations), GPU begins demonstrate advantages 1.3x speedup, computation time becomes significant transfer overhead. larger datasets (1000+ observations), GPU significantly faster (2-10x speedup expected) computational cost dominates fixed memory transfer overhead. observation consistent GPU computing literature: GPUs excel large-scale intensive computations, CPUs remain competitive smaller tasks data transfer overhead non-negligible.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Results 70% train / 30% test split evaluation.

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Train MSE consistently lower test MSE indicates proper normalization absence overfitting GPU CPU produce numerically identical predictions across configurations Consistent 50-86% improvement baseline across hyperparameter settings Different kernels learning rates provide tuning flexibility

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: Hardware Support: - Automatic GPU/CPU detection via device=“auto” - Explicit device control via device=“cpu” device=“cuda” - Graceful fallback CPU GPU unavailable Numerical Methods: - Automatic feature normalization zero mean unit variance - Gaussian likelihood proper variance handling - Numerical stability appropriate scaling Kernel Options: - RBF (Radial Basis Function) kernel smooth functions - Matern kernel functions controlled smoothness - Configurable via kernel hyperparameter

title: GPyTorch Learner for mlr3
text: CPU-usage: - Modern multi-core CPU - 4GB RAM minimum GPU acceleration: - NVIDIA GPU CUDA support - CUDA Toolkit 11.8 12.x - 4GB GPU memory recommended - cuDNN library (installed PyTorch)
